blocks.html_en: Are companies actually seeing massive productivity gains from their AI adoption? Does any of this sordid affair make sense?

sentences.en: Are companies actually seeing massive productivity gains from their AI adoption?
sentences.zh: 公司是否真的通过人工智能的采用而实现了生产力的巨大提升？

sentences.en: Does any of this sordid affair make sense?
sentences.zh: 这件肮脏的事情还有道理吗？

blocks.html_en: This should be an easy question, but it is surprisingly hard to get a straight answer to it. Executives that tell the press that their company has gone insane will quickly find themselves removed from their positions. Employees who are honest will find themselves fired in short-order, or “randomly” selected for a round of layoffs. In fact, it is in the interests of almost every actor in the space – boards, executives, employees, vendors, consultants – to obfuscate and misrepresent the success rate of AI projects. Many publicly traded companies are putting out announcements about their AI productivity gains when I know for a fact that the businesses have done nothing other than purchase Copilot licenses and declare victory.

sentences.en: This should be an easy question, but it is surprisingly hard to get a straight answer to it.
sentences.zh: 这应该是一个简单的问题，但要得到直接答案却出人意料地困难。

sentences.en: Executives that tell the press that their company has gone insane will quickly find themselves removed from their positions.
sentences.zh: 告诉媒体他们的公司已经疯了的高管很快就会发现自己被解雇了。

sentences.en: Employees who are honest will find themselves fired in short-order, or “randomly” selected for a round of layoffs.
sentences.zh: 诚实的员工会发现自己很快被解雇，或者“随机”被选中进行一轮裁员。

sentences.en: In fact, it is in the interests of almost every actor in the space – boards, executives, employees, vendors, consultants – to obfuscate and misrepresent the success rate of AI projects.
sentences.zh: 事实上，混淆和歪曲人工智能项目的成功率符合该领域几乎所有参与者（董事会、高管、员工、供应商、顾问）的利益。

sentences.en: Many publicly traded companies are putting out announcements about their AI productivity gains when I know for a fact that the businesses have done nothing other than purchase Copilot licenses and declare victory.
sentences.zh: 许多上市公司都在发布有关人工智能生产力提升的公告，而我知道这些公司除了购买 Copilot 许可证并宣布胜利之外什么也没做。

blocks.html_en: Yet we need to know if these projects are panning out – if the total focus on AI as a core tenet of business strategy is succeeding at a reasonable rate, then a discussion about the relative risk and reward is warranted.

sentences.en: Yet we need to know if these projects are panning out – if the total focus on AI as a core tenet of business strategy is succeeding at a reasonable rate, then a discussion about the relative risk and reward is warranted.
sentences.zh: 然而，我们需要知道这些项目是否正在取得成功——如果将人工智能作为商业战略核心原则的总体关注以合理的速度取得成功，那么就有必要讨论相对风险和回报。

blocks.html_en: Unfortunately, we live in a dark timeline. All of the AI projects we have observed as a team are failing. Every single one – we have seen 0% success in a year and a half, not only amongst projects we have been asked to participate in2, but even within projects that we have observed in passing while doing totally unrelated work. Even if you grant that AI tooling accelerates specific workloads, the method and scale of the current investments is senseless. Frequently the failure is not related to AI itself, but rather that companies are terminally bad at running software projects effectively, and as I have remarked previously, AI projects are subject to all the failure modes of normal projects plus you can get everything right and then still fail because of the method's novelty. Very few companies are so good at shipping software that they can afford the extra risk profile.

sentences.en: Unfortunately, we live in a dark timeline.
sentences.zh: 不幸的是，我们生活在一个黑暗的时间线中。

sentences.en: All of the AI projects we have observed as a team are failing.
sentences.zh: 我们作为一个团队观察到的所有人工智能项目都失败了。

sentences.en: Every single one – we have seen 0% success in a year and a half, not only amongst projects we have been asked to participate in2, but even within projects that we have observed in passing while doing totally unrelated work.
sentences.zh: 每一个项目——我们在一年半的时间里都看到了 0% 的成功，不仅是在我们被要求参与的项目中，甚至在我们在做完全不相关的工作时顺便观察到的项目中也是如此。

sentences.en: Even if you grant that AI tooling accelerates specific workloads, the method and scale of the current investments is senseless.
sentences.zh: 即使你承认人工智能工具可以加速特定的工作负载，当前投资的方法和规模也是毫无意义的。

sentences.en: Frequently the failure is not related to AI itself, but rather that companies are terminally bad at running software projects effectively, and as I have remarked previously, AI projects are subject to all the failure modes of normal projects plus you can get everything right and then still fail because of the method's novelty.
sentences.zh: 通常，失败与人工智能本身无关，而是公司在有效运行软件项目方面表现不佳，正如我之前所说，人工智能项目会受到正常项目的所有失败模式的影响，而且你可能把一切都做好了，但由于方法的新颖性，仍然会失败。

sentences.en: Very few companies are so good at shipping software that they can afford the extra risk profile.
sentences.zh: 很少有公司如此擅长交付软件，以至于能够承受额外的风险。

blocks.html_en: Often enough, though, it’s an actual failure in what LLMs can accomplish. The most common version of this, being rolled out across businesses around the world, is the internally-facing chatbot, or for the more daring company, the customer-facing chatbot. The story is always the same. For the former, I’ve never seen substantial internal uptake from inside a business. Employees don’t use internal chatbots because companies tend to have low-quality documentation and an LLM is not psychic – it can only know things that have been written down and made accessible. For the latter customer-facing applications, I have rarely had a pleasant experience as a consumer, with perhaps the exception of live transcription during medical appointments – hardly something worth pivoting an entire organisation around. In both cases, project leaders are very careful to avoid tracking basic metrics, such as whether the tools are being used at all, or they track metrics that are easily gamed.

sentences.en: Often enough, though, it’s an actual failure in what LLMs can accomplish.
sentences.zh: 然而，通常情况下，法学硕士的成就实际上是失败的。

sentences.en: The most common version of this, being rolled out across businesses around the world, is the internally-facing chatbot, or for the more daring company, the customer-facing chatbot.
sentences.zh: 最常见的版本是面向内部的聊天机器人，在世界各地的企业中推广，或者对于更大胆的公司来说，是面向客户的聊天机器人。

sentences.en: The story is always the same.
sentences.zh: 故事总是一样的。

sentences.en: For the former, I’ve never seen substantial internal uptake from inside a business.
sentences.zh: 对于前者，我从未见过企业内部有大量的内部吸收。

sentences.en: Employees don’t use internal chatbots because companies tend to have low-quality documentation and an LLM is not psychic – it can only know things that have been written down and made accessible.
sentences.zh: 员工不使用内部聊天机器人，因为公司往往拥有低质量的文档，而且法学硕士没有通灵能力——它只能知道已经写下来并可以访问的东西。

sentences.en: For the latter customer-facing applications, I have rarely had a pleasant experience as a consumer, with perhaps the exception of live transcription during medical appointments – hardly something worth pivoting an entire organisation around.
sentences.zh: 对于后者面向客户的应用程序，我作为消费者很少有愉快的体验，也许除了医疗预约期间的实时转录之外——这几乎不值得整个组织围绕。

sentences.en: In both cases, project leaders are very careful to avoid tracking basic metrics, such as whether the tools are being used at all, or they track metrics that are easily gamed.
sentences.zh: 在这两种情况下，项目负责人都非常小心地避免跟踪基本指标，例如是否正在使用这些工具，或者他们跟踪容易被操纵的指标。

blocks.html_en: For example, my last consumer interaction was attempting to get help from Mitsubishi following an automotive failure, where a very polite robot asked me to describe the problem and that I’d receive a call back as soon as someone was available. This was the single most competent implementation of such a project I’ve seen in the wild, in that the voice was natural sounding, responded quickly, was clearly “live” in production, and promised a swift resolution.

sentences.en: For example, my last consumer interaction was attempting to get help from Mitsubishi following an automotive failure, where a very polite robot asked me to describe the problem and that I’d receive a call back as soon as someone was available.
sentences.zh: 例如，我上次的消费者互动是在汽车故障后试图向三菱寻求帮助，其中一个非常有礼貌的机器人要求我描述问题，并且一旦有人有空，我就会收到回电。

sentences.en: This was the single most competent implementation of such a project I’ve seen in the wild, in that the voice was natural sounding, responded quickly, was clearly “live” in production, and promised a swift resolution.
sentences.zh: 这是我在野外见过的此类项目的最有能力的实施，因为声音听起来自然，响应迅速，显然是“现场”生产，并承诺迅速解决。

blocks.html_en: That was six months ago, and I did not, in fact, get a call back.

sentences.en: That was six months ago, and I did not, in fact, get a call back.
sentences.zh: 那是六个月前的事了，事实上我没有接到回电。

blocks.html_en: When Mitsubishi did not call me back, what happened? Did that request just go into the void, showing one less incident for the year? Does it appear that the phone bot resolved my query without the need for human intervention? All we know is that it didn’t show up as an error, or I’d have received a call. I’m sure it looks great in all sorts of ways except the one that matters, which is that I was planning to buy a car and decided not to buy another one of theirs.

sentences.en: When Mitsubishi did not call me back, what happened?
sentences.zh: 当三菱没有给我回电话时，发生了什么？

sentences.en: Did that request just go into the void, showing one less incident for the year?
sentences.zh: 难道这个请求就这样落空了，导致今年少了一起事故吗？

sentences.en: Does it appear that the phone bot resolved my query without the need for human intervention?
sentences.zh: 电话机器人是否无需人工干预就解决了我的问题？

sentences.en: All we know is that it didn’t show up as an error, or I’d have received a call.
sentences.zh: 我们所知道的是，它没有显示为错误，否则我会接到电话。

sentences.en: I’m sure it looks great in all sorts of ways except the one that matters, which is that I was planning to buy a car and decided not to buy another one of theirs.
sentences.zh: 我确信它在各个方面看起来都很棒，除了重要的一点，那就是我本来打算买一辆车，但决定不再购买他们的另一辆车。

blocks.html_en: For this reason, our team has quickly learned while on an engagement not to ask anything about ongoing AI projects in any context – by the time that project has started, it is too late for the management team, and intervention is not possible until a crisis point is inevitably reached. There is no conceivable positive outcome. The failure rate is so high that even basic inquiry leaves us in an untenable position. Any coherent question about how it’s going, what the goal is, who is using it, constitutes an inadvertent attack on the chain of command responsible for the work because there are no good answers to anything. Even in rare cases where my interlocutor has stated that things are going well (usually while the project is still mid-flight and failure has not had a chance to manifest), it is generally obvious that they are doomed, but at least in these cases I can simply agree and then go home to scream into a pillow for six hours straight3.

sentences.en: For this reason, our team has quickly learned while on an engagement not to ask anything about ongoing AI projects in any context – by the time that project has started, it is too late for the management team, and intervention is not possible until a crisis point is inevitably reached.
sentences.zh: 出于这个原因，我们的团队很快就学会了在参与过程中不要在任何情况下询问有关正在进行的人工智能项目的任何信息——当项目开始时，对于管理团队来说已经太晚了，除非不可避免地达到危机点，否则不可能进行干预。

sentences.en: There is no conceivable positive outcome.
sentences.zh: 没有可想象的积极结果。

sentences.en: The failure rate is so high that even basic inquiry leaves us in an untenable position.
sentences.zh: 失败率如此之高，以至于即使是基本的调查也让我们陷入了难以为继的境地。

sentences.en: Any coherent question about how it’s going, what the goal is, who is using it, constitutes an inadvertent attack on the chain of command responsible for the work because there are no good answers to anything.
sentences.zh: 任何关于它如何进行、目标是什么、谁在使用它的连贯问题，都会构成对负责这项工作的指挥链的无意攻击，因为任何事情都没有好的答案。

sentences.en: Even in rare cases where my interlocutor has stated that things are going well (usually while the project is still mid-flight and failure has not had a chance to manifest), it is generally obvious that they are doomed, but at least in these cases I can simply agree and then go home to scream into a pillow for six hours straight3.
sentences.zh: 即使在极少数情况下，我的对话者表示事情进展顺利（通常当项目仍在进行中，失败还没有机会显现时），通常很明显他们注定要失败，但至少在这些情况下，我可以简单地同意，然后回家连续六个小时在枕头上尖叫。

blocks.html_en: All of this is to say that I am very confident that almost every report at a company about “massive AI productivity gains” is untrue as a matter of brute fact. Even if some companies are seeing clear gains, this is the exception, not the norm. With that assumption in place, we can talk about the dynamics at play, and how it has become impossible for many organisations to stay focused on things that actually matter to their long-term (or even short-term) health.

sentences.en: All of this is to say that I am very confident that almost every report at a company about “massive AI productivity gains” is untrue as a matter of brute fact.
sentences.zh: 所有这一切都表明，我非常有信心，公司中几乎所有关于“人工智能生产力大幅提升”的报告本质上都是不真实的。

sentences.en: Even if some companies are seeing clear gains, this is the exception, not the norm.
sentences.zh: 即使一些公司看到了明显的收益，但这也是例外，而不是常态。

sentences.en: With that assumption in place, we can talk about the dynamics at play, and how it has become impossible for many organisations to stay focused on things that actually matter to their long-term (or even short-term) health.
sentences.zh: 有了这个假设，我们就可以讨论其中的动态，以及许多组织如何无法专注于对其长期（甚至短期）健康真正重要的事情。

blocks.html_en: II. Heretics Will Be Shot

sentences.en: II.
sentences.zh: 二.

sentences.en: Heretics Will Be Shot
sentences.zh: 异教徒将被枪决

blocks.html_en: It has become outright dangerous to even raise the possibility that AI might not be the solution to a problem, let alone be the sole focus of a company’s entire strategy.

sentences.en: It has become outright dangerous to even raise the possibility that AI might not be the solution to a problem, let alone be the sole focus of a company’s entire strategy.
sentences.zh: 即使提出人工智能可能无法解决问题的可能性，也已经变得非常危险，更不用说成为公司整个战略的唯一焦点了。

blocks.html_en: In every sufficiently large business we have observed (say, with 500+ employees), we have noted that continued advancement, and increasingly continued employment, has started to require repeated professions of belief in the transformative power of AI for said business. I am not talking about providing ideas about how to use AI in the business – I mean religious profession, declarations of faith. Overwhelmingly these statements are made by non-technicians, though it is not uncommon for technicians to emit deranged statements to curry favour.

sentences.en: In every sufficiently large business we have observed (say, with 500+ employees), we have noted that continued advancement, and increasingly continued employment, has started to require repeated professions of belief in the transformative power of AI for said business.
sentences.zh: 在我们观察到的每一个足够大的企业（例如，拥有 500 多名员工）中，我们注意到，持续的进步和越来越多的持续就业，已经开始要求人们不断地相信人工智能对上述企业的变革力量。

sentences.en: I am not talking about providing ideas about how to use AI in the business – I mean religious profession, declarations of faith.
sentences.zh: 我并不是在谈论提供如何在商业中使用人工智能的想法——我指的是宗教职业、信仰宣言。

sentences.en: Overwhelmingly these statements are made by non-technicians, though it is not uncommon for technicians to emit deranged statements to curry favour.
sentences.zh: 这些言论绝大多数是由非技术人员发表的，尽管技术人员为了讨好而发表疯狂言论的情况并不少见。

blocks.html_en: There have been several occasions where I have seen someone, apropos of nothing, blurt out almost word-for-word “AI is changing everything”, only to concede moments later that their organisation does not currently use LLMs for anything, and indeed, that they cannot name a single thing that has changed other than they get some use out of ChatGPT (frequently the free-tier). In one extreme case, I have seen an executive confess that they had never even used ChatGPT or any AI tool in their life, immediately after producing a technical strategy for an organisation with $2B+ in revenue which was entirely centered around AI.

sentences.en: There have been several occasions where I have seen someone, apropos of nothing, blurt out almost word-for-word “AI is changing everything”, only to concede moments later that their organisation does not currently use LLMs for anything, and indeed, that they cannot name a single thing that has changed other than they get some use out of ChatGPT (frequently the free-tier).
sentences.zh: 有好几次，我看到有人毫无缘由地脱口而出，几乎逐字逐句地说“人工智能正在改变一切”，但过了一会儿才承认他们的组织目前没有使用法学硕士来做任何事情，事实上，他们除了从 ChatGPT（通常是免费套餐）中得到一些使用之外，无法说出任何已经发生变化的事情。

sentences.en: In one extreme case, I have seen an executive confess that they had never even used ChatGPT or any AI tool in their life, immediately after producing a technical strategy for an organisation with $2B+ in revenue which was entirely centered around AI.
sentences.zh: 在一个极端的例子中，我看到一位高管承认，在为一家收入超过 20 亿美元、完全以人工智能为中心的组织制定技术战略后，他们一生中从未使用过 ChatGPT 或任何人工智能工具。

blocks.html_en: Initially these statements were so absurd on their face that I thought it was some cynical ploy to achieve thought leader status, and there are certainly some people doing this – I have had it admitted to me. But the broader reality is so much worse: people who have no background in the technology at all actually believe what they are saying. As a general rule you should avoid getting into business with a liar, but if you must, you can at least reason with them even if only in private. A true believer is much more threatening because they are impervious to even inducement by self-interest.

sentences.en: Initially these statements were so absurd on their face that I thought it was some cynical ploy to achieve thought leader status, and there are certainly some people doing this – I have had it admitted to me.
sentences.zh: 起初，这些言论在他们看来是如此荒谬，以至于我认为这是一些为了获得思想领袖地位而愤世嫉俗的策略，而且肯定有人这样做——我已经承认了这一点。

sentences.en: But the broader reality is so much worse: people who have no background in the technology at all actually believe what they are saying.
sentences.zh: 但更广泛的现实要糟糕得多：根本没有技术背景的人实际上相信他们所说的。

sentences.zh: 一般来说，您应该避免与骗子打交道，但如果必须的话，您至少可以与他们讲道理，即使只是私下讲道理。

sentences.en: A true believer is much more threatening because they are impervious to even inducement by self-interest.
sentences.zh: 真正的信徒更具威胁性，因为他们甚至不会受到自身利益的诱惑。

blocks.html_en: The turning point in my belief was watching someone with a spectacular amount of money on the line fire their highest performers because they were achieving that performance without LLMs. When an employer publicly talks about AI innovation, we have to ask ourselves if they’re simply trying to manipulate the market or customers. When they privately commit to strategies like this with their own money at stake, with no attempt to communicate that strategy to external clients, I can only assume they really mean what they’re saying.

sentences.en: The turning point in my belief was watching someone with a spectacular amount of money on the line fire their highest performers because they were achieving that performance without LLMs.
sentences.zh: 我信念的转折点是看到一个拥有巨额资金的人解雇了他们表现最好的员工，因为他们在没有法学硕士的情况下实现了这种表现。

sentences.en: When an employer publicly talks about AI innovation, we have to ask ourselves if they’re simply trying to manipulate the market or customers.
sentences.zh: 当雇主公开谈论人工智能创新时，我们必须问自己，他们是否只是想操纵市场或客户。

sentences.en: When they privately commit to strategies like this with their own money at stake, with no attempt to communicate that strategy to external clients, I can only assume they really mean what they’re saying.
sentences.zh: 当他们用自己的钱私下承诺实施这样的策略，而不试图向外部客户传达该策略时，我只能假设他们是真心实意的。

blocks.html_en: A while ago, I wrote “Contra Ptacek’s Terrible Article On AI”, which was focused on the fact that many of Ptacek’s points in his own essay “My AI Skeptic Friends Are All Nuts” were internally inconsistent4. But on the crux of the matter, we are actually in total agreement, because he opens his essay with this:

blocks.html_en: Which is to say that we can sidestep arguments about the precise utility of LLMs entirely and we’re left in a very simple place – it is entirely obvious to both myself and Ptacek, two people that are coming at this from fairly opposed views, that people are being really, really stupid about this, and that organisations are demanding bizarre workflow constraints from their specialist staff.5

sentences.en: Which is to say that we can sidestep arguments about the precise utility of LLMs entirely and we’re left in a very simple place – it is entirely obvious to both myself and Ptacek, two people that are coming at this from fairly opposed views, that people are being really, really stupid about this, and that organisations are demanding bizarre workflow constraints from their specialist staff.5

blocks.html_en: These mandates have led to extremely strange places. Several of my peers now “AI-wash” their work, meaning that even when they can perfectly competently execute on their jobs to the satisfaction of their management teams, said managers are unhappy if the engineers haven’t used AI in the work… so now they’re lying about using LLMs even in contexts where their professional judgement is that they aren’t the appropriate tool. They just do the work, the same way they have for decades, and say Claude did it. Others are being measured on their AI bills with “token leaderboards”, where higher is better because I have evidently fallen into the pocket of Hell where the demons torment me by doing elaborate impressions of absolute fucking morons, so the people hired for their freakish ability to perform system optimisation do the obvious thing. They set the LLMs prompting themselves in a semi-plausible loop in case someone inspects the token consumption and then they watch Netflix. Not a single one has been caught, even when their own assessment of the output is that it isn’t suitable for deployment.

blocks.html_en: III. AI Demos Are The Mind-Killer

sentences.en: III.
sentences.zh: 三．

sentences.en: AI Demos Are The Mind-Killer
sentences.zh: 人工智能演示是精神杀手

blocks.html_en: Bless me, Father, for I have sinned. It has been ∞ days since my last confession. I accuse myself of the following sins:

sentences.en: Bless me, Father, for I have sinned.
sentences.zh: 父啊，请保佑我，因为我犯了罪。

sentences.en: It has been ∞ days since my last confession.
sentences.zh: 距离我上次告白已经过去了 ∞ 天。

sentences.en: I accuse myself of the following sins:
sentences.zh: 我控诉自己犯有以下罪孽：

blocks.html_en: One of the main pieces of infrastructure we deploy at our clients is an analytics-focused database called Snowflake – for a typical business, the bill is tiny because it’s a pay-as-you-go situation and we can process all their data in one minute a day, you get a very hands-off deployment, and in short it has many characteristics that are very pleasant for our work. One of the features in Snowflake that we don’t use is called Cortex.

sentences.en: One of the main pieces of infrastructure we deploy at our clients is an analytics-focused database called Snowflake – for a typical business, the bill is tiny because it’s a pay-as-you-go situation and we can process all their data in one minute a day, you get a very hands-off deployment, and in short it has many characteristics that are very pleasant for our work.
sentences.zh: 我们在客户处部署的主要基础设施之一是一个名为 Snowflake 的以分析为中心的数据库——对于典型的企业来说，费用很小，因为它是按需付费的情况，我们可以每天一分钟处理他们的所有数据，你会得到一个非常不干涉的部署，总之它具有许多对我们的工作非常有利的特性。

sentences.en: One of the features in Snowflake that we don’t use is called Cortex.
sentences.zh: Snowflake 中我们不使用的功能之一称为 Cortex。

blocks.html_en: Cortex is their AI chatbot layer, with the ability to plug into metadata (for non-nerds, descriptions of your data, like what a column in a spreadsheet means) and query a company’s database autonomously. In theory, you can ask a question like “What was our revenue for last week?” and it will spit out an answer.

sentences.en: Cortex is their AI chatbot layer, with the ability to plug into metadata (for non-nerds, descriptions of your data, like what a column in a spreadsheet means) and query a company’s database autonomously.
sentences.zh: Cortex 是他们的人工智能聊天机器人层，能够插入元数据（对于非书呆子来说，是数据的描述，就像电子表格中的列的含义）并自主查询公司的数据库。

sentences.en: In theory, you can ask a question like “What was our revenue for last week?”
sentences.zh: 理论上，你可以问“我们上周的收入是多少？”之类的问题。

sentences.en: and it will spit out an answer.
sentences.zh: 它会吐出一个答案。

blocks.html_en: It is not really suitable for production usage. From memory, the last time I was given a presentation on it, by actual Snowflake staff, they reported that ideal configuration results in something like ~92% accuracy due to the complexity of data at a large business (see: probably best-in-class for these tools, but imagine your CFO having one in every ten of their numbers be outright wrong) and there were serious issues with managing deployments. Nonetheless, it can be used to produce some very flashy demonstrations.

sentences.en: It is not really suitable for production usage.
sentences.zh: 它并不真正适合生产使用。

sentences.en: From memory, the last time I was given a presentation on it, by actual Snowflake staff, they reported that ideal configuration results in something like ~92% accuracy due to the complexity of data at a large business (see: probably best-in-class for these tools, but imagine your CFO having one in every ten of their numbers be outright wrong) and there were serious issues with managing deployments.
sentences.zh: 据我记忆，上次我在 Snowflake 的实际工作人员那里进行演示时，他们报告说，由于大型企业数据的复杂性，理想的配置会带来约 92% 的准确率（请参阅：这些工具可能是同类中最好的，但想象一下，您的 CFO 十分之一的数字是完全错误的），并且在管理部署方面存在严重问题。

sentences.en: Nonetheless, it can be used to produce some very flashy demonstrations.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，它可以用来制作一些非常华丽的演示。

blocks.html_en: On several occasions, we’ve been exposed to folks that have been sort of lukewarm on our main offerings, but they really, really wanted to use AI to perform a natural language query on their data. And we thought “Okay, if you really want to see it, maybe we can caveat this appropriately and show you what it might look like.”

sentences.en: On several occasions, we’ve been exposed to folks that have been sort of lukewarm on our main offerings, but they really, really wanted to use AI to perform a natural language query on their data.
sentences.zh: 有几次，我们遇到了一些对我们的主要产品不冷不热的人，但他们真的非常想使用人工智能对他们的数据执行自然语言查询。

sentences.en: And we thought “Okay, if you really want to see it, maybe we can caveat this appropriately and show you what it might look like.”
sentences.zh: 我们想：“好吧，如果你真的想看它，也许我们可以适当地警告一下，并向你展示它可能是什么样子。”

blocks.html_en: This was a terrible mistake. It backfired in the most predictable way imaginable – every lukewarm client that saw the chatbot in action, even with us telling them that it was not going to accomplish what they wanted, wanted to buy it immediately. Every other consideration, including millions of dollars that we could plausibly help them achieve by non-AI means, was swept aside. It was like a dark and terrible force seized control of their limbs, plunged their hands into their own chests, and presented their still-beating credit cards to us in grim supplication. We were so mortified by the inexplicable shift in energy that we (wisely) declined to take the money and ended the sales process, and soon thereafter removed Cortex from our list of demonstrations. It would have been too irresponsible to exploit this gap in their reasoning, and frankly, it was already irresponsible to have even run the demonstration – doctors don’t walk around showing off cool pills that they’d never prescribe.

sentences.en: This was a terrible mistake.
sentences.zh: 这是一个可怕的错误。

sentences.en: It backfired in the most predictable way imaginable – every lukewarm client that saw the chatbot in action, even with us telling them that it was not going to accomplish what they wanted, wanted to buy it immediately.
sentences.zh: 它以可以想象到的最可预测的方式适得其反——每一个看到聊天机器人运行的冷淡客户，即使我们告诉他们它不会实现他们想要的目标，也想立即购买它。

sentences.en: Every other consideration, including millions of dollars that we could plausibly help them achieve by non-AI means, was swept aside.
sentences.zh: 所有其他考虑因素，包括我们可以通过非人工智能方式帮助他们实现的数百万美元，都被抛在了一边。

sentences.en: It was like a dark and terrible force seized control of their limbs, plunged their hands into their own chests, and presented their still-beating credit cards to us in grim supplication.
sentences.zh: 就像一股黑暗而可怕的力量控制了他们的四肢，将他们的双手插入自己的胸口，并向我们展示了仍在跳动的信用卡，以严峻的祈求。

sentences.en: We were so mortified by the inexplicable shift in energy that we (wisely) declined to take the money and ended the sales process, and soon thereafter removed Cortex from our list of demonstrations.
sentences.zh: 我们对这种莫名其妙的能量变化感到非常羞愧，所以我们（明智地）拒绝接受这笔钱并结束了销售过程，并很快将 Cortex 从我们的演示列表中删除了。

sentences.en: It would have been too irresponsible to exploit this gap in their reasoning, and frankly, it was already irresponsible to have even run the demonstration – doctors don’t walk around showing off cool pills that they’d never prescribe.
sentences.zh: 利用他们推理中的这一缺陷就太不负责任了，坦率地说，甚至进行演示就已经是不负责任的了——医生不会到处炫耀他们从未开过的很酷的药片。

blocks.html_en: Watching the total 180°, that shift from ice-cold to red-hot buying frenzy, was a deeply unsettling experience. It was personally uncomfortable to see people that clearly didn’t gel with us interpersonally suddenly dying to enter an ongoing relationship, but more broadly uncomfortable because for a brief moment I began to understand what is happening in sales meetings around the world. There was no warning I could have given that would have made them refuse to buy the damn thing – their appetite was as large as their budget could stretch, and some part of me wonders if this is because they knew that their ravenous hunger would be present in their own customers. They’d just buy it from us, then pivot right to a larger company and mind control their leadership team until the buck finally stops with the loser that needs to justify the expense. The main protection against this seems to be that the median vendor is so bad at their jobs that we had presented the first even somewhat-working products these people had seen, and this included an ASX-listed company that was already bragging about their AI usage. It took our team two hours to produce something that was frankly not that good – basically just typing text descriptions of data into a web browser – and it was still better than anything the leads had seen because they had nothing to show for all the investment.

sentences.zh: 目睹整个 180° 的转变，从冰冷的购买狂潮到炽热的购买狂潮，是一次令人深感不安的经历。

sentences.zh: 防止这种情况发生的主要措施似乎是中位供应商的工作表现非常糟糕，以至于我们向他们展示了他们见过的第一个甚至有点工作的产品，其中包括一家在澳大利亚证券交易所上市的公司，该公司已经在吹嘘他们的人工智能使用情况。

sentences.en: It took our team two hours to produce something that was frankly not that good – basically just typing text descriptions of data into a web browser – and it was still better than anything the leads had seen because they had nothing to show for all the investment.
sentences.zh: 我们的团队花了两个小时才制作出一些坦率地说不是那么好的东西——基本上只是在网络浏览器中输入数据的文本描述——但它仍然比领导者看到的任何东西都要好，因为他们没有任何东西可以展示所有的投资。

blocks.html_en: In fact, we have been forced to opt out of every sale where the lead has expressed anything beyond the most fleeting curiosity in the use of AI in their business. I don’t mean that we’ve heard that they’re interested in AI and elected to drop the contract on moral grounds. I mean that, over the course of the engagement, these people have exhibited a pattern of behavior that has made it near-impossible to sell to them without incurring reputational and legal risk, and are furthermore crafting management environments that I can only describe as cultish, ineffective, and “please dear God, do not let it be on earth as it is on LinkedIn”.

sentences.en: In fact, we have been forced to opt out of every sale where the lead has expressed anything beyond the most fleeting curiosity in the use of AI in their business.
sentences.zh: 事实上，我们被迫退出每一次销售，只要销售线索表达了对人工智能在其业务中的使用的最短暂的好奇心之外的任何内容。

sentences.en: I don’t mean that we’ve heard that they’re interested in AI and elected to drop the contract on moral grounds.
sentences.zh: 我并不是说我们听说他们对人工智能感兴趣并出于道德原因选择放弃合同。

sentences.en: I mean that, over the course of the engagement, these people have exhibited a pattern of behavior that has made it near-impossible to sell to them without incurring reputational and legal risk, and are furthermore crafting management environments that I can only describe as cultish, ineffective, and “please dear God, do not let it be on earth as it is on LinkedIn”.
sentences.zh: 我的意思是，在参与的过程中，这些人表现出了一种行为模式，几乎不可能在不承担声誉和法律风险的情况下向他们销售产品，而且他们正在营造一种我只能形容为邪教、低效的管理环境，“请亲爱的上帝，不要让它像 LinkedIn 上那样出现在地球上”。

sentences.en: Executives, Game Theory, and The Emperor’s Clothes

blocks.html_en:
The good news is, CISOs are used to having to protect the business from their hare-brained initiatives, and this one isn’t really that different, except that there’s a cult-like atmosphere to it that you didn’t see with, say, the cloud. It almost doesn’t matter whether you embrace the initiative or not; there’s work to be done to manage the risk, so that’s what you do. From talking to CISOs everywhere, I would say most of them are quietly skeptical but afraid to speak up.

– Career CISO and well-known speaker that asked to remain anonymous

blocks.html_en: Despite the substantial prevalence of true believers, many of the people running large AI initiatives, or making public statements about them, do not believe what they are saying. There are “heads of AI” who read this blog, at companies with $1B+ in annually recurring revenue, who have written in to say they believe their job is totally fraudulent but it was the only promotion pathway remaining at the organisation.

sentences.en: Despite the substantial prevalence of true believers, many of the people running large AI initiatives, or making public statements about them, do not believe what they are saying.
sentences.zh: 尽管真正的信徒相当普遍，但许多实施大型人工智能计划或对其发表公开声明的人并不相信他们所说的。

sentences.en: There are “heads of AI” who read this blog, at companies with $1B+ in annually recurring revenue, who have written in to say they believe their job is totally fraudulent but it was the only promotion pathway remaining at the organisation.
sentences.zh: 有些“人工智能负责人”在年经常性收入超过 10 亿美元的公司读过这篇博客，他们写信表示，他们认为自己的工作完全是欺诈性的，但这是该组织剩下的唯一晋升途径。

blocks.html_en: On a trip overseas, I had the privilege of a meeting with one of the Fortune 500 executives mentioned at the beginning of the post, who will remain anonymous so that they are not executed by firing squad by their board. As we were chatting, it became clear that they were very switched-on and technically competent, and they also happened to be at a company that had committed to the usual battery of exorbitant claims about their recent innovations – we’ve 100x’d our productivity, AI is the future of everything, I am but a vessel for OpenAI to make love to my wife. You know, normal things. But since I had them there without any microphones around, I asked why this was being repeated without opposition. Was it just sales fluff?

sentences.en: On a trip overseas, I had the privilege of a meeting with one of the Fortune 500 executives mentioned at the beginning of the post, who will remain anonymous so that they are not executed by firing squad by their board.
sentences.zh: 在一次海外旅行中，我有幸会见了本文开头提到的一位财富 500 强高管，他将保持匿名，以免被董事会行刑队处决。

sentences.en: As we were chatting, it became clear that they were very switched-on and technically competent, and they also happened to be at a company that had committed to the usual battery of exorbitant claims about their recent innovations – we’ve 100x’d our productivity, AI is the future of everything, I am but a vessel for OpenAI to make love to my wife.
sentences.zh: 在我们聊天的过程中，我们发现他们非常活跃，技术能力也很强，而且他们所在的公司也恰好对他们最近的创新做出了一系列夸张的宣称——我们的生产力提高了 100 倍，人工智能是一切的未来，我只是 OpenAI 与我妻子做爱的一个容器。

sentences.en: You know, normal things.
sentences.zh: 你知道，很正常的事情。

sentences.en: But since I had them there without any microphones around, I asked why this was being repeated without opposition.
sentences.zh: 但由于我让他们在那里没有任何麦克风，我问为什么这会在没有反对的情况下重复进行。

sentences.en: Was it just sales fluff?
sentences.zh: 难道这只是销售噱头吗？

blocks.html_en: The answer was a lot more interesting. It was partially ridiculous sales material being delivered to an easily excitable audience, but this was not the dominant factor constraining honesty. Executives at their customers were saying absurd things about achieving 100x productivity, and this meant that if any executive at the vendor said that these gains were not plausible, it would undermine the credibility of the customer’s executive, be perceived as an attack (or heresy), and possibly result in an enterprise contract cancellation. And getting enterprise contracts cancelled because you wanted to opine on something that doesn’t really matter to your organisation’s mission is a great way to get fired.

sentences.en: The answer was a lot more interesting.
sentences.zh: 答案更有趣。

sentences.en: It was partially ridiculous sales material being delivered to an easily excitable audience, but this was not the dominant factor constraining honesty.
sentences.zh: 向容易兴奋的受众提供部分荒谬的销售材料，但这并不是限制诚实的主导因素。

sentences.en: Executives at their customers were saying absurd things about achieving 100x productivity, and this meant that if any executive at the vendor said that these gains were not plausible, it would undermine the credibility of the customer’s executive, be perceived as an attack (or heresy), and possibly result in an enterprise contract cancellation.
sentences.zh: 客户的高管们对实现 100 倍的生产力提出了荒谬的说法，这意味着如果供应商的任何高管表示这些收益不合理，就会损害客户高管的可信度，被视为攻击（或异端邪说），并可能导致企业合同取消。

sentences.en: And getting enterprise contracts cancelled because you wanted to opine on something that doesn’t really matter to your organisation’s mission is a great way to get fired.
sentences.zh: 因为你想对一些对你的组织使命并不重要的事情发表意见而取消企业合同，这是一种被解雇的好方法。

blocks.html_en: But this company was also a major player, of the kind that signs enormous enterprise contracts with other companies. So presumably there is another vendor that has sold to them, and their CEO is worried that saying something sane will contradict this executive, and very quickly we can see how we can have executives around the world nervously pointing guns at each other, not wanting to be shot first but also watching everything gradually spiral out of control6. This is to say that we’re facing a coordination problem around executives being honest around the AI gains they’ve witnessed – if they co-operate, they keep their jobs. If they defect, they will possibly be fired by their embarrassed peers (who have now been implicitly called liars, cowards, or incompetents) and then replaced with someone that will toe the line anyway. If they could all admit the truth at once there might be some hope, but there is no way to coordinate that event.

sentences.en: But this company was also a major player, of the kind that signs enormous enterprise contracts with other companies.
sentences.zh: 但这家公司也是一个主要参与者，与其他公司签署了巨额企业合同。

sentences.en: So presumably there is another vendor that has sold to them, and their CEO is worried that saying something sane will contradict this executive, and very quickly we can see how we can have executives around the world nervously pointing guns at each other, not wanting to be shot first but also watching everything gradually spiral out of control6.
sentences.zh: 因此，想必还有另一家供应商向他们出售了产品，他们的首席执行官担心说一些理智的话会与这位高管相矛盾，很快我们就可以看到世界各地的高管如何紧张地用枪指着对方，不想先被枪杀，但又看着一切逐渐失控6。

sentences.en: This is to say that we’re facing a coordination problem around executives being honest around the AI gains they’ve witnessed – if they co-operate, they keep their jobs.
sentences.zh: 这就是说，我们面临着一个协调问题，即高管们是否要诚实地对待他们所目睹的人工智能成果——如果他们合作，他们就保住工作。

sentences.en: If they defect, they will possibly be fired by their embarrassed peers (who have now been implicitly called liars, cowards, or incompetents) and then replaced with someone that will toe the line anyway.
sentences.zh: 如果他们叛逃，他们可能会被尴尬的同事解雇（他们现在被含蓄地称为骗子、胆小鬼或无能者），然后被无论如何都会遵守规则的人取代。

sentences.en: If they could all admit the truth at once there might be some hope, but there is no way to coordinate that event.
sentences.zh: 如果他们都能同时承认真相，也许还有一些希望，但没有办法协调这一事件。

blocks.html_en: This sounds deeply concerning, but it is worth noting that it means that some executives who are emitting nonsensical statements are not as dull as they might seem at first – they’re in a fraught political environment, where they are surrounded by many people that are gunning for their roles, and subject to the whims of a board that is undergoing similar pressure. Against all the dictates of reason, I have presented on navigating AI hype to people on S&P 500 boards7 and they are in exactly the same situation – the main comments I remember from the session were board members admitting they were skeptical, but expressing anxiety that their positions were contingent on demanding AI investment. One of them commented “investing this early seems like risk without much upside”. About two years later, I can see now that their decade-old multi-billion dollar organisation is now branded as “AI-native”, whatever the hell that means.

sentences.en: This sounds deeply concerning, but it is worth noting that it means that some executives who are emitting nonsensical statements are not as dull as they might seem at first – they’re in a fraught political environment, where they are surrounded by many people that are gunning for their roles, and subject to the whims of a board that is undergoing similar pressure.
sentences.zh: 这听起来令人深感担忧，但值得注意的是，这意味着一些发表无意义言论的高管并不像乍看起来那么迟钝——他们身处一个令人担忧的政治环境，周围有许多争夺自己职位的人，并且受到承受类似压力的董事会的突发奇想。

sentences.en: Against all the dictates of reason, I have presented on navigating AI hype to people on S&P 500 boards7 and they are in exactly the same situation – the main comments I remember from the session were board members admitting they were skeptical, but expressing anxiety that their positions were contingent on demanding AI investment.
sentences.zh: 与所有理性的要求相反，我向标准普尔 500 指数董事会的成员介绍了如何引导人工智能炒作7，他们的情况完全相同——我记得会议上的主要评论是董事会成员承认他们持怀疑态度，但表达了他们的立场取决于对人工智能投资的要求的焦虑。

sentences.en: One of them commented “investing this early seems like risk without much upside”.
sentences.zh: 其中一位评论道，“这么早就投资似乎是有风险的，没有太大的上升空间”。

sentences.en: About two years later, I can see now that their decade-old multi-billion dollar organisation is now branded as “AI-native”, whatever the hell that means.
sentences.zh: 大约两年后，我现在可以看到，他们拥有十年历史、价值数十亿美元的组织现在被贴上了“人工智能原生”的标签，不管这意味着什么。

blocks.html_en: V. You Must Be This AI-Native To Ride

sentences.en: V.
sentences.zh: 五、

sentences.en: You Must Be This AI-Native To Ride
sentences.zh: 你必须是人工智能原生者才能骑行

blocks.html_en: All of the above converges on the state that we find ourselves in now, where effective decisionmaking has ground to a halt. Collectively, what started as a few people undergoing either destabilising psychological events or being caught up in hype has now resulted in an environment where leaders cannot speak honestly about their beliefs on how best to guide organisations, for fear of being removed, creating a sort of distributed government by assassination. This means that the least sensible recommendations are going totally unchallenged, resulting in employees being evaluated on totally gameable metrics such as “money spent on AI”, and those employees must play along to avoid being terminated. This has also created an insatiable appetite for purchasing “AI” solutions, which target both true believers that will believe implausible claims, and also non-believers that cannot decline the purchases without having their commitment to the cause coming into question.

sentences.en: All of the above converges on the state that we find ourselves in now, where effective decisionmaking has ground to a halt.
sentences.zh: 所有上述情况都集中在我们现在所处的状态，有效的决策已经陷入停滞。

sentences.en: Collectively, what started as a few people undergoing either destabilising psychological events or being caught up in hype has now resulted in an environment where leaders cannot speak honestly about their beliefs on how best to guide organisations, for fear of being removed, creating a sort of distributed government by assassination.
sentences.zh: 总的来说，最初是少数人经历了破坏稳定的心理事件或陷入了炒作，现在已经导致了这样一种环境：领导人无法诚实地谈论他们关于如何最好地指导组织的信念，因为担心被罢免，从而通过暗杀创建了一种分布式政府。

sentences.en: This means that the least sensible recommendations are going totally unchallenged, resulting in employees being evaluated on totally gameable metrics such as “money spent on AI”, and those employees must play along to avoid being terminated.
sentences.zh: 这意味着最不明智的建议完全不会受到质疑，导致员工根据完全可玩的指标（例如“花在人工智能上的钱”）进行评估，而这些员工必须配合以避免被解雇。

sentences.en: This has also created an insatiable appetite for purchasing “AI” solutions, which target both true believers that will believe implausible claims, and also non-believers that cannot decline the purchases without having their commitment to the cause coming into question.
sentences.zh: 这也引发了人们对购买“人工智能”解决方案的贪得无厌的胃口，这些解决方案既针对那些会相信难以置信的主张的真正信徒，也针对那些在不对事业的承诺受到质疑的情况下无法拒绝购买的非信徒。

blocks.html_en: This means that all offers that are subject to internal politics at an ideologically captured organisation must include AI alignment, even if the value proposition is patently ambiguous. My assessment of the market so far is that a substantial component of the outburst of AI projects are actually non-AI projects with an AI element slapped on after the fact to pass the purity test.

sentences.en: This means that all offers that are subject to internal politics at an ideologically captured organisation must include AI alignment, even if the value proposition is patently ambiguous.
sentences.zh: 这意味着，在一个意识形态占据的组织中，所有受内部政治影响的提议都必须包括人工智能的一致性，即使价值主张显然是模棱两可的。

sentences.en: My assessment of the market so far is that a substantial component of the outburst of AI projects are actually non-AI projects with an AI element slapped on after the fact to pass the purity test.
sentences.zh: 到目前为止，我对市场的评估是，人工智能项目爆发的很大一部分实际上是非人工智能项目，只是为了通过纯度测试而添加了人工智能元素。

blocks.html_en: For example, I recently witnessed an organisation handling a database migration from an Oracle database to Snowflake – instead of handling the migration directly, the vendor bolted on a preliminary phase which involved trying to get an LLM to automate the translation of the Oracle-flavored SQL to Snowflake-flavored SQL. When the project failed (due to issues getting enough permissions to automate the work, not because an LLM can’t do something that easy), the vendor simply started handling the translation by hand but the company billed it as an AI-driven success because some inconsequential portion of the SQL had been translated by AI before being pasted over.

sentences.en: For example, I recently witnessed an organisation handling a database migration from an Oracle database to Snowflake – instead of handling the migration directly, the vendor bolted on a preliminary phase which involved trying to get an LLM to automate the translation of the Oracle-flavored SQL to Snowflake-flavored SQL.
sentences.zh: 例如，我最近目睹了一个组织处理从 Oracle 数据库到 Snowflake 的数据库迁移 - 供应商没有直接处理迁移，而是选择了一个初步阶段，其中包括尝试让 LLM 自动将 Oracle 风格的 SQL 转换为 Snowflake 风格的 SQL。

sentences.en: When the project failed (due to issues getting enough permissions to automate the work, not because an LLM can’t do something that easy), the vendor simply started handling the translation by hand but the company billed it as an AI-driven success because some inconsequential portion of the SQL had been translated by AI before being pasted over.
sentences.zh: 当项目失败时（由于获得足够的权限来自动化工作的问题，而不是因为法学硕士不能做那么简单的事情），供应商只是开始手动处理翻译，但该公司将其称为人工智能驱动的成功，因为 SQL 的一些无关紧要的部分在粘贴之前已经由人工智能翻译了。

blocks.html_en: What was actually purchased? A totally standard database migration to help an executive meet the strategic deliverable of decommissioning a system prior to license renewal. What was sold to their superiors? “I allocated a substantial percentage of my budget to AI and it helped me accomplish my mandate.” True AI projects, of the kind that is driven by an LLM as the sole mechanism underlying it, where the project can clearly fail to deliver specific numbers, are actually very rare. We mostly see them in the context of startups, and frankly we have stopped engaging with them because we kept getting to the end of the sales conversation and finding out they wanted us to build the product that they were marketing as completed.

sentences.en: What was actually purchased?
sentences.zh: 实际购买了什么？

sentences.en: A totally standard database migration to help an executive meet the strategic deliverable of decommissioning a system prior to license renewal.
sentences.zh: 完全标准的数据库迁移，可帮助管理人员在许可证续订之前实现停用系统的战略交付成果。

sentences.en: What was sold to their superiors?
sentences.zh: 卖给上级的是什么？

sentences.en: “I allocated a substantial percentage of my budget to AI and it helped me accomplish my mandate.”
sentences.zh: “我将很大一部分预算分配给了人工智能，它帮助我完成了我的任务。”

sentences.en: True AI projects, of the kind that is driven by an LLM as the sole mechanism underlying it, where the project can clearly fail to deliver specific numbers, are actually very rare.
sentences.zh: 真正的人工智能项目，由法学硕士作为其唯一机制驱动的那种，项目显然无法提供具体的数字，实际上非常罕见。

sentences.en: We mostly see them in the context of startups, and frankly we have stopped engaging with them because we kept getting to the end of the sales conversation and finding out they wanted us to build the product that they were marketing as completed.
sentences.zh: 我们主要在初创公司的背景下看到他们，坦率地说，我们已经停止与他们接触，因为我们一直在销售对话结束时发现他们希望我们构建他们正在营销的产品。

blocks.html_en: However, some projects simply do not have an easy way to tack on the AI label, or the person advocating for them either does not want to lie or has not understood that lying has become necessary. In all cases, this either kills the request for funding outright, or adds a pervasive and intractable drag on all communications, as every request must be worked and re-worked until it is “AI enough”. Failure to comply will either result in denial or, in many cases, a demand from a true believer to know why the extra work “can’t be done with AI”. Many companies have actively publicized that this is their new hiring policy – when a member of staff requests additional headcount, they must demonstrate that they have tried to use AI first. The part that’s being left out is that if you say you used AI and still need the help, you will be labelled “bad at AI” and potentially laid off.

sentences.en: However, some projects simply do not have an easy way to tack on the AI label, or the person advocating for them either does not want to lie or has not understood that lying has become necessary.
sentences.zh: 然而，有些项目根本没有一种简单的方法来贴上人工智能标签，或者倡导者要么不想撒谎，要么不明白撒谎已经成为必要。

sentences.en: In all cases, this either kills the request for funding outright, or adds a pervasive and intractable drag on all communications, as every request must be worked and re-worked until it is “AI enough”.
sentences.zh: 在所有情况下，这要么彻底终止资金请求，要么给所有通信增加普遍且棘手的阻力，因为每个请求都必须经过反复处理，直到“足够人工智能”为止。

sentences.en: Failure to comply will either result in denial or, in many cases, a demand from a true believer to know why the extra work “can’t be done with AI”.
sentences.zh: 如果不遵守，要么会被拒绝，要么在许多情况下，真正的信徒会要求知道为什么额外的工作“不能用人工智能来完成”。

sentences.en: Many companies have actively publicized that this is their new hiring policy – when a member of staff requests additional headcount, they must demonstrate that they have tried to use AI first.
sentences.zh: 许多公司都积极宣传这是他们的新招聘政策——当员工要求增加员工人数时，他们必须证明他们已经尝试过首先使用人工智能。

sentences.en: The part that’s being left out is that if you say you used AI and still need the help, you will be labelled “bad at AI” and potentially laid off.
sentences.zh: 被遗漏的部分是，如果你说你使用了人工智能但仍然需要帮助，你将被贴上“不擅长人工智能”的标签，并可能被解雇。

blocks.html_en: The net result of this is that almost every large organisation that I am aware of is no longer able to focus on anything important, unless they are one of the (very) few organisations where AI happens to address their highest priorities. They cannot buy sensible software, hire competent talent, communicate honestly with executives about the state of projects, or undertake any sort of sensible initiative.

sentences.en: The net result of this is that almost every large organisation that I am aware of is no longer able to focus on anything important, unless they are one of the (very) few organisations where AI happens to address their highest priorities.
sentences.zh: 这样做的最终结果是，据我所知，几乎每个大型组织都不再能够专注于任何重要的事情，除非它们是人工智能碰巧解决其最高优先级的（非常）少数组织之一。

sentences.en: They cannot buy sensible software, hire competent talent, communicate honestly with executives about the state of projects, or undertake any sort of sensible initiative.
sentences.zh: 他们无法购买明智的软件、雇用有能力的人才、与高管就项目状态进行诚实沟通或采取任何明智的举措。

sentences.en: VI.

sentences.en: Navigating AI Mania
sentences.zh: 驾驭人工智能狂热

blocks.html_en: – So Sick, Domesticated Incels

sentences.en: – So Sick, Domesticated Incels
sentences.zh: – 病态的，驯养的乱伦

blocks.html_en: This is an unfortunate situation to be in, but it will pass eventually. I’ve learned a lot about the latent insanity that we have inculcated in our leadership strata, and unfortunately those traits will persist long past the current bubble, merely awaiting another similar reactivation trigger – and some organisations will stay captured until they have totally collapsed, in the way that not everyone has successfully moved away from the dreadful blockchain affair. That’s something to write about for another time.

sentences.en: This is an unfortunate situation to be in, but it will pass eventually.
sentences.zh: 这是一个不幸的情况，但它最终会过去。

sentences.en: I’ve learned a lot about the latent insanity that we have inculcated in our leadership strata, and unfortunately those traits will persist long past the current bubble, merely awaiting another similar reactivation trigger – and some organisations will stay captured until they have totally collapsed, in the way that not everyone has successfully moved away from the dreadful blockchain affair.
sentences.zh: 我学到了很多关于我们在领导层中灌输的潜在疯狂的知识，不幸的是，这些特征将在当前的泡沫过后很长一段时间内持续存在，只是等待另一个类似的重新激活触发因素——一些组织将一直被困住，直到完全崩溃，就像并不是每个人都成功地摆脱了可怕的区块链事件一样。

sentences.en: That’s something to write about for another time.
sentences.zh: 这是下次再写的事情。

blocks.html_en: What I wanted to get to were some thoughts on surviving the immediate crisis, either by directly making systemic improvements or by holding onto your sanity. I’ll start with the “making improvements” part, because that’s the situation I find myself in the most frequently.

sentences.en: What I wanted to get to were some thoughts on surviving the immediate crisis, either by directly making systemic improvements or by holding onto your sanity.
sentences.zh: 我想要得到的是关于如何度过当前危机的一些想法，要么直接进行系统性改进，要么保持理智。

sentences.en: I’ll start with the “making improvements” part, because that’s the situation I find myself in the most frequently.
sentences.zh: 我将从“做出改进”部分开始，因为这是我最常遇到的情况。

blocks.html_en: When You Have Another Objective

sentences.en: When You Have Another Objective
sentences.zh: 当你有另一个目标时

blocks.html_en: We’re going to do a lot of sucking it up and smiling here. This section assumes that you are trying to achieve some goal that isn't repairing the organisation's manic stance, but either trying to course-correct a specific project (and possibly risk getting fired as either a leader or consultant) or achieve some totally unrelated goal.

sentences.en: We’re going to do a lot of sucking it up and smiling here.
sentences.zh: 我们会在这里做很多忍气吞声和微笑的事情。

sentences.en: This section assumes that you are trying to achieve some goal that isn't repairing the organisation's manic stance, but either trying to course-correct a specific project (and possibly risk getting fired as either a leader or consultant) or achieve some totally unrelated goal.
sentences.zh: 本节假设您正在尝试实现一些目标，但不是修复组织的躁狂立场，而是尝试纠正特定项目的方向（并且可能冒着被解雇为领导者或顾问的风险）或实现一些完全不相关的目标。

blocks.html_en: • Where possible, when raising issues, do not have conversations about the state of AI projects in group settings, as this creates a dynamic where each individual member of the group is worried about outing themselves in front of their peers. Arrange for one-on-one settings. Make it clear that you are willing to countenance that the current AI environment is frothy, and that you will keep opinions unidentifiable when raising them elsewhere. Be extremely aware that the most outspoken people can be identified by their peers, so take care to avoid exposing your sources by, e.g. direct quotes. In the event that only a small minority (say, one person in a group of six people) is willing to speak out, it might be worth giving up and moving on to a patient that has better chances.

sentences.en: Where possible, when raising issues, do not have conversations about the state of AI projects in group settings, as this creates a dynamic where each individual member of the group is worried about outing themselves in front of their peers.
sentences.zh: 在可能的情况下，在提出问题时，不要在小组环境中讨论人工智能项目的状态，因为这会产生一种动态，使小组中的每个成员都担心在同事面前暴露自己。

sentences.en: Arrange for one-on-one settings.
sentences.zh: 安排一对一的设置。

sentences.en: Make it clear that you are willing to countenance that the current AI environment is frothy, and that you will keep opinions unidentifiable when raising them elsewhere.
sentences.zh: 明确表示你愿意接受当前人工智能环境的泡沫，并且在其他地方提出意见时你将保持无法识别的观点。

sentences.en: Be extremely aware that the most outspoken people can be identified by their peers, so take care to avoid exposing your sources by, e.g. direct quotes.
sentences.zh: 请务必注意，最直言不讳的人可以被同行识别出来，因此请注意避免通过以下方式暴露您的消息来源。直接引用。

sentences.en: In the event that only a small minority (say, one person in a group of six people) is willing to speak out, it might be worth giving up and moving on to a patient that has better chances.
sentences.zh: 如果只有一小部分人（例如，六人小组中的一个人）愿意说出来，那么可能值得放弃并转向有更好机会的患者。

blocks.html_en: • For ongoing projects, an effective trick that I believe I picked up from Secrets of Consulting is the anonymous poll, where you can ask individuals to rate their opinion of an AI project’s success chances on a scale of 1 to 10. The typical split I have observed is half of those involved rating the project at a 3/10 and others at around an 8/10 – a clear bimodal split on a project that was already three years late. Bringing this data to a CEO can be an effective method of pointing out that some information is clearly being hidden from them on the state of the project.

sentences.en: For ongoing projects, an effective trick that I believe I picked up from Secrets of Consulting is the anonymous poll, where you can ask individuals to rate their opinion of an AI project’s success chances on a scale of 1 to 10.
sentences.zh: 对于正在进行的项目，我相信我从《咨询的秘密》中学到的一个有效技巧是匿名民意调查，你可以要求个人以 1 到 10 的范围来评价他们对人工智能项目成功机会的看法。

sentences.en: The typical split I have observed is half of those involved rating the project at a 3/10 and others at around an 8/10 – a clear bimodal split on a project that was already three years late.
sentences.zh: 我观察到的典型分裂是一半参与评价该项目的人为 3/10，其他人则为 8/10 左右——对于一个已经晚了三年的项目来说，这是一个明显的双峰分裂。

sentences.en: Bringing this data to a CEO can be an effective method of pointing out that some information is clearly being hidden from them on the state of the project.
sentences.zh: 将这些数据提供给首席执行官可能是一种有效的方法，可以向他们指出一些关于项目状态的信息显然被隐藏了。

blocks.html_en: • Always involve people on the ground. The only source of data on whether projects are succeeding or the investment is going anywhere are the people that use it for their day-to-day activity. Care must be taken to bring them into the environment where they are treated with respect (all sufficiently large companies have people that view subordinates as not-quite-real-people). It is not uncommon to uncover worldview-shaking information in short order – with one client, we uncovered that staff were totally unaware they had been given licenses for AI tooling, which cast into doubt all productivity claims.

sentences.en: Always involve people on the ground.
sentences.zh: 始终让实地人员参与进来。

sentences.en: The only source of data on whether projects are succeeding or the investment is going anywhere are the people that use it for their day-to-day activity.
sentences.zh: 关于项目是否成功或投资是否到位的唯一数据来源是在日常活动中使用它的人。

sentences.en: Care must be taken to bring them into the environment where they are treated with respect (all sufficiently large companies have people that view subordinates as not-quite-real-people).
sentences.zh: 必须小心地将他们带入一个受到尊重的环境中（所有足够大的公司都有人将下属视为不完全真实的人）。

sentences.en: It is not uncommon to uncover worldview-shaking information in short order – with one client, we uncovered that staff were totally unaware they had been given licenses for AI tooling, which cast into doubt all productivity claims.
sentences.zh: 短时间内发现颠覆世界观的信息并不罕见——在一位客户身上，我们发现员工完全不知道他们已经获得了人工智能工具的许可证，这让所有生产力主张都受到质疑。

blocks.html_en: • Do not question the broadest claims about AI. I cannot emphasize this enough. If someone says “AI is changing everything”, just let it pass if your goal is to fix an object-level problem rather than challenge the reality at the institution. The challenge can only come after you have gained the trust of the most senior person involved. Trust is gained over a meal in private where you assuage their anxieties, not by embarrassing them in front of peers.

sentences.en: Do not question the broadest claims about AI.
sentences.zh: 不要质疑有关人工智能的最广泛的主张。

sentences.en: I cannot emphasize this enough.
sentences.zh: 我怎么强调都不为过。

sentences.en: If someone says “AI is changing everything”, just let it pass if your goal is to fix an object-level problem rather than challenge the reality at the institution.
sentences.zh: 如果有人说“人工智能正在改变一切”，如果你的目标是解决对象级问题而不是挑战机构的现实，那就让它过去吧。

sentences.en: The challenge can only come after you have gained the trust of the most senior person involved.
sentences.zh: 只有在您获得最高级别人员的信任后，挑战才会到来。

sentences.en: Trust is gained over a meal in private where you assuage their anxieties, not by embarrassing them in front of peers.
sentences.zh: 信任是通过私下吃饭来缓解他们的焦虑而获得的，而不是通过在同事面前让他们难堪来获得。

blocks.html_en: • Remember that you do not know what statements have been emitted prior to entering a room. There will sometimes be people that have publicly committed to statements like “I am 100x more productive than I was last year”, and some may even wish they hadn’t said that but are too embarrassed to walk it back. In an untested room, common sense like “LLMs should not be allowed to deploy code without human review” can kill your chances to make an impact before you’ve even started.

sentences.en: Remember that you do not know what statements have been emitted prior to entering a room.
sentences.zh: 请记住，您不知道在进入房间之前发出了什么声明。

sentences.en: There will sometimes be people that have publicly committed to statements like “I am 100x more productive than I was last year”, and some may even wish they hadn’t said that but are too embarrassed to walk it back.
sentences.zh: 有时会有人公开承诺诸如“我的工作效率比去年提高了 100 倍”之类的言论，有些人甚至希望自己没有说过这句话，但又不好意思收回来。

sentences.en: In an untested room, common sense like “LLMs should not be allowed to deploy code without human review” can kill your chances to make an impact before you’ve even started.
sentences.zh: 在未经测试的房间中，诸如“法学硕士不应在未经人工审核的情况下部署代码”之类的常识可能会在您开始之前就扼杀您产生影响的机会。

blocks.html_en: • My practice requires me to maintain an honest relationship with my clients or the whole thing falls apart, so I can’t do this – but honestly, if you work in the fire service and need money to stop a puppy from catching fire, just lie. It’s fine. History will forgive you. Add a $10,000 AI chatbot to your project, exclusively discuss that part in meetings, whatever. Save that puppy.

sentences.en: My practice requires me to maintain an honest relationship with my clients or the whole thing falls apart, so I can’t do this – but honestly, if you work in the fire service and need money to stop a puppy from catching fire, just lie.
sentences.zh: 我的实践要求我与客户保持诚实的关系，否则整个事情就会崩溃，所以我不能这样做——但说实话，如果你在消防部门工作并且需要钱来阻止小狗着火，那就撒谎吧。

sentences.en: It’s fine.
sentences.zh: 没关系。

sentences.en: History will forgive you.
sentences.zh: 历史会原谅你的。

sentences.en: Add a $10,000 AI chatbot to your project, exclusively discuss that part in meetings, whatever.
sentences.zh: 在您的项目中添加一个价值 10,000 美元的人工智能聊天机器人，在会议中专门讨论该部分，等等。

sentences.en: Save that puppy.
sentences.zh: 救救那只小狗。

blocks.html_en: When You're Just Trying To Survive

sentences.en: When You're Just Trying To Survive
sentences.zh: 当你只是想生存时

blocks.html_en: This is for people that are just waiting for the bubble to burst and trying not to go nuts.

sentences.en: This is for people that are just waiting for the bubble to burst and trying not to go nuts.
sentences.zh: 这是为那些只是等待泡沫破灭并努力不发疯的人准备的。

blocks.html_en: • I have bad news – accept that you are probably not going to meaningfully push back on any of this. This is not a feature of AI, it’s a feature of dysfunctional companies.

sentences.en: I have bad news – accept that you are probably not going to meaningfully push back on any of this.
sentences.zh: 我有一个坏消息——接受你可能不会对这一切进行有意义的反击。

sentences.en: This is not a feature of AI, it’s a feature of dysfunctional companies.
sentences.zh: 这不是人工智能的特征，而是功能失调的公司的特征。

blocks.html_en: • If you feel like you’re going absolutely nuts, consider switching over to contracting. I’ve advocated for contracting many times over full-time employment, but you’ll get paid a lot more and be left out of most internal politics. Also when you run into a really intolerable situation, you’ll know that you’ve got a fixed end-date.

sentences.en: If you feel like you’re going absolutely nuts, consider switching over to contracting.
sentences.zh: 如果你觉得自己快要疯了，可以考虑改用合同制。

sentences.en: I’ve advocated for contracting many times over full-time employment, but you’ll get paid a lot more and be left out of most internal politics.
sentences.zh: 我曾多次主张与全职工作签订合同，但你会得到更多的报酬，并且被排除在大多数内部政治之外。

sentences.en: Also when you run into a really intolerable situation, you’ll know that you’ve got a fixed end-date.
sentences.zh: 此外，当你遇到真正无法忍受的情况时，你会知道你有一个固定的结束日期。

blocks.html_en: • I do my best to limit my uptake of AI-related news, as it is pretty crazy-making and unproductive to consume. I no longer visit Hackernews, Reddit, or really anywhere where I am going to be drip-fed nonsense, though I allow myself exceptions for very funny things like Apple suing OpenAI over alleged corporate espionage. Consume exactly the amount you need to feel like you aren’t going insane, then stop. Ditto for complaining with friends – and tell them that’s why you’re talking about it, which buys a lot of tolerance.

sentences.en: I do my best to limit my uptake of AI-related news, as it is pretty crazy-making and unproductive to consume.
sentences.zh: 我尽最大努力限制对人工智能相关新闻的接收，因为它非常疯狂，而且消费起来毫无效率。

sentences.en: I no longer visit Hackernews, Reddit, or really anywhere where I am going to be drip-fed nonsense, though I allow myself exceptions for very funny things like Apple suing OpenAI over alleged corporate espionage.
sentences.zh: 我不再访问 Hackernews、Reddit，或者任何我会被灌输废话的地方，尽管我允许自己例外一些非常有趣的事情，比如苹果起诉 OpenAI 涉嫌企业间谍活动。

sentences.en: Consume exactly the amount you need to feel like you aren’t going insane, then stop.
sentences.zh: 消耗的量恰好是您感觉自己不会发疯所需的量，然后停止。

sentences.en: Ditto for complaining with friends – and tell them that’s why you’re talking about it, which buys a lot of tolerance.
sentences.zh: 向朋友抱怨也是如此——告诉他们这就是你谈论这件事的原因，这会带来很大的宽容。

blocks.html_en: • When someone tells me they are using AI for something when they really shouldn’t be, I smile and nod as long as they are unlikely to get themselves killed. Even family. Especially family.

sentences.en: When someone tells me they are using AI for something when they really shouldn’t be, I smile and nod as long as they are unlikely to get themselves killed.
sentences.zh: 当有人告诉我他们正在使用人工智能做一些不应该做的事情时，我会微笑点头，只要他们不太可能被杀。

sentences.en: Even family.
sentences.zh: 甚至是家人。

sentences.en: Especially family.
sentences.zh: 尤其是家庭。

blocks.html_en: • When someone asks me for my opinion of AI as a programmer, I recommend saying “Oh, that stuff is pretty overblown” and then changing the topic, unless they are in a position where their opinion might influence something important. Non-programmers need this guidance the most.

sentences.en: When someone asks me for my opinion of AI as a programmer, I recommend saying “Oh, that stuff is pretty overblown” and then changing the topic, unless they are in a position where their opinion might influence something important.
sentences.zh: 当有人问我作为一名程序员对人工智能的看法时，我建议说“哦，那东西太夸张了”，然后改变话题，除非他们的观点可能会影响一些重要的事情。

sentences.en: Non-programmers need this guidance the most.
sentences.zh: 非程序员最需要这种指导。

blocks.html_en: • If you’re being asked to review huge volumes of terrible AI code, just assume that the organisation is going to burn you out and fire you. You will not convince the person drowning you in 2000 line PRs to stop. Start looking for a new job as if you have already been fired. I have seen this happen many times now, and it always plays out the same way – do the job search while you have energy. Don’t worry if your speed drops or management gets annoyed at you. There is no way to avoid that, you can simply choose whether it happens now because of your job search, or later because you are too depressed to work anymore.

sentences.en: If you’re being asked to review huge volumes of terrible AI code, just assume that the organisation is going to burn you out and fire you.
sentences.zh: 如果你被要求审查大量糟糕的人工智能代码，就假设该组织会耗尽你的精力并解雇你。

sentences.en: You will not convince the person drowning you in 2000 line PRs to stop.
sentences.zh: 你无法说服那个在 2000 行 PR 中淹没你的人停下来。

sentences.en: Start looking for a new job as if you have already been fired.
sentences.zh: 开始寻找新工作，就像您已经被解雇一样。

sentences.en: I have seen this happen many times now, and it always plays out the same way – do the job search while you have energy.
sentences.zh: 我已经多次看到这种情况发生了，而且总是以同样的方式进行——趁你有精力的时候去找工作。

sentences.en: Don’t worry if your speed drops or management gets annoyed at you.
sentences.zh: 如果您的速度下降或管理层对您感到恼火，请不要担心。

sentences.en: There is no way to avoid that, you can simply choose whether it happens now because of your job search, or later because you are too depressed to work anymore.
sentences.zh: 没有办法避免这种情况，你可以简单地选择是现在因为你找工作而发生，还是以后因为你太沮丧而无法再工作了。

blocks.html_en: • If your manager is responding to you with clearly AI-generated text, use AI to respond to save your sanity and then look for a new job. Many people assume they will get in trouble for being that obviously rude. You will not, this particular behavior is exhibited only by true believers, and they actually like that you’ve clearly not bothered to engage with them. I know, it’s fucking wild.

sentences.en: If your manager is responding to you with clearly AI-generated text, use AI to respond to save your sanity and then look for a new job.
sentences.zh: 如果您的经理用人工智能生成的清晰文本回复您，请使用人工智能进行回复以保持理智，然后寻找新工作。

sentences.en: Many people assume they will get in trouble for being that obviously rude.
sentences.zh: 许多人认为他们会因为如此粗鲁而惹上麻烦。

sentences.en: You will not, this particular behavior is exhibited only by true believers, and they actually like that you’ve clearly not bothered to engage with them.
sentences.zh: 你不会的，这种特殊的行为只有真正的信徒才会表现出来，他们实际上喜欢你显然没有费心与他们接触。

sentences.en: I know, it’s fucking wild.
sentences.zh: 我知道，这太疯狂了。

blocks.html_en: • If you’re being asked to max out on token usage, look for a new j – okay look, you get it, right? Go find a job that isn’t going to wrench reality from your tenuous grasp. They do exist, largely at companies so small that they don’t turn up on job platforms. It might take months to find one, so start now.

sentences.en: If you’re being asked to max out on token usage, look for a new j – okay look, you get it, right?
